hero.title: Hi, I'm Ahmad — an AI Application Developer.
hero.description: I build production AI products end-to-end — ML models, LLM/RAG systems, and the full-stack apps around them.

aboutSection.label: profile.sys / decode
aboutSection.title: The engineer
aboutSection.intro: AI Application Developer combining a ZAB-recognised Bachelor in AI Engineering with 3+ years of full-stack experience. I build AI products end-to-end — machine learning, computer vision (OpenCV, Keras, Grad-CAM), NLP, and LLM assistants — and ship them as production apps (Python/FastAPI, Next.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL). I care about shipping real, usable AI products, not notebooks.
story: From AI engineering studies to production systems — I combine ML model work with the full-stack delivery that turns research into software people actually use.
Recent focus: computer vision with explainability (Grad-CAM), RAG assistants with source citations, and context-grounded LLM integrations in real web apps.
I'm seeking a full-time role where I can build AI products end-to-end — from model and retrieval layer to API, UI, and deployment.

principles.title: Own the outcome
principles.body: From schema design to UI polish — I follow problems end-to-end instead of throwing them over the wall.

principles.title: Ship to learn
principles.body: Small, reliable releases beat big-bang demos — I iterate with metrics, feedback, and room to improve.

projects.title: ASD Early-Detection Platform
projects.des: Health-tech AI platform: CNN image classifier (MobileNetV3) with Grad-CAM explainability, ML questionnaire (~92%), and a RAG assistant (fastembed embeddings + semantic search over WHO/CDC, LLM generation, source citations). Next.js + FastAPI, bilingual AR/EN.
projects.challenge: Accessible early screening with explainable AI — not a black-box label
projects.solution: Three-layer AI health app: CNN + Grad-CAM, ML questionnaire, and RAG assistant with citations
projects.overview: An end-to-end AI health application with three AI layers: (1) a CNN image classifier (fine-tuned MobileNetV3) returning Grad-CAM heatmaps for explainability; (2) an ML behavioural-questionnaire classifier (neural network, ~92% accuracy) with structured reports; (3) a RAG assistant with multilingual semantic retrieval (fastembed embeddings + cosine-similarity search) over a verified medical knowledge base (WHO/CDC), an LLM (Hugging Face) for generation, and answers with source citations plus an anti-hallucination fallback.
projects.challengeDetail: Early autism signs are easy to miss without specialist access, and many AI demos stop at a classification label. The product needed three AI layers — visual screening, behavioural questionnaire, and a trustworthy Q&A assistant — with explainable outputs, clear medical disclaimers, and a bilingual UX that works in production.
projects.solutionDetail: Built as a monorepo — Next.js 15 (Vercel) frontend with AR/EN i18n (RTL/LTR), FastAPI backend (Hugging Face Spaces) with Docker, health checks, CORS and Swagger. The image pipeline uses fine-tuned MobileNetV3 with Grad-CAM heatmaps. The questionnaire path uses a trained neural network (~92% accuracy). The RAG layer uses fastembed embeddings, cosine-similarity retrieval over WHO/CDC sources, Hugging Face LLM generation, source citations, and anti-hallucination fallback. Privacy-first (in-memory processing) with clear medical disclaimers.
keyFeatures: CNN image classifier — fine-tuned MobileNetV3 with Grad-CAM heatmaps for explainability
ML questionnaire classifier — neural network (~92% accuracy) with structured reports
RAG assistant — fastembed embeddings, cosine-similarity search, WHO/CDC knowledge base, source citations
Next.js 15 App Router — AR/EN i18n with RTL/LTR, image upload, questionnaire, about pages
FastAPI backend on Hugging Face Spaces — Docker, health checks, CORS, Swagger docs
Privacy-first — in-memory processing with clear medical disclaimers
outcomes: Live demo at autism-spectrum-six.vercel.app with open GitHub monorepo
Three AI layers — computer vision, ML questionnaire, and RAG assistant with citations
13 UI screens documented — home, detect, results, about, bilingual flows
Production deployment — Vercel frontend + Hugging Face Docker API with CORS

projects.title: "Ask Ahmad" — Portfolio AI Assistant
projects.des: A context-grounded LLM assistant embedded in this site, answering recruiter questions from portfolio data. Multi-provider fallback chain (Groq, Gemini, OpenAI, Mistral, OpenRouter), anti-hallucination prompting, bilingual EN/DE. NOT vector-RAG — context-grounded.
projects.challenge: Recruiters need quick, accurate answers about experience — without digging through every page
projects.solution: Context-grounded LLM chat widget with multi-provider fallback and strict anti-hallucination prompting
projects.overview: An LLM assistant (the chat widget on this site) that answers questions about my projects, experience and availability, grounded strictly in my portfolio data. Built with a multi-provider fallback chain (Groq → Gemini → OpenAI → Mistral → OpenRouter) for reliability and cost control, strict anti-hallucination system prompting (answers only from provided context), short-term conversation memory, and bilingual EN/DE responses. Next.js Route Handler (/api/chat) calling OpenAI-compatible and Gemini endpoints via fetch.
projects.challengeDetail: Portfolio sites are static — recruiters and hiring managers often want quick answers about stack fit, project depth, and availability without reading every case study. The assistant had to stay honest (no invented experience), work reliably when one API provider fails, and respond in English or German.
projects.solutionDetail: I built a Next.js /api/chat Route Handler that injects portfolio content as system context, enforces answers-only-from-context rules, and tries providers in priority order until one succeeds. Short-term conversation memory keeps follow-up questions coherent. Try it — the assistant in the corner is this project.
keyFeatures: Multi-provider fallback — Groq → Gemini → OpenAI → Mistral → OpenRouter
Context-grounded prompting — answers only from portfolio data (not vector RAG)
Anti-hallucination guardrails — refuses when context is insufficient
Bilingual EN/DE responses aligned with site locale
Next.js Route Handler — OpenAI-compatible + Gemini fetch integration
Short-term conversation memory for natural follow-ups
outcomes: Try it — the assistant in the corner is this project
5-provider fallback chain for reliability and cost control
Strict grounding in CMS content — no invented projects or metrics
Open-source implementation in this Next.js codebase

metrics.label: Embedded chat widget on this site

projects.title: VoteChain — Decentralized Voting dApp
projects.des: Full Web3 election platform — immutable votes on Ethereum, candidate/voter media on IPFS, MetaMask integration, and a live results dashboard with role-based admin.
projects.challenge: Centralized voting can be altered — elections need transparent, tamper-proof records
projects.solution: Hybrid Web3 dApp: Solidity smart contract + IPFS storage + Next.js admin & voter UI
projects.overview: Personal portfolio project — Web3 dApp for transparent elections (built outside full-time employment). Votes are counted immutably on Ethereum (Solidity + Hardhat); candidate metadata on IPFS. Next.js frontend with MetaMask, role-based admin, and live results.
projects.challengeDetail: Traditional voting systems rely on a central authority that can alter records. The product needed clear on-chain rules (one vote per registered voter, organizer-only registration), decentralized media storage without expensive on-chain blobs, CORS-safe IPFS uploads from the browser, and a polished UX so non-Web3 users can follow the flow — confirm modals, toasts, loaders, and readable live results.
projects.solutionDetail: I built a full-stack Web3 app: the Create smart contract (Solidity 0.8.9, OpenZeppelin Counters) enforces organizer permissions, voter whitelisting, and single-vote integrity. Next.js API routes (/api/ipfs-upload, /api/ipfs-proxy) handle server-side IPFS uploads and image proxying. VotingContext centralizes Web3 state (Ethers.js v5, Web3Modal, MetaMask). OrganizerGuard mirrors contract rules in the UI. React Dropzone feeds IPFS; live dashboards read vote counts directly from the chain after each transaction.
keyFeatures: Smart contract — setCandidate(), voterRight(), vote() with events and on-chain RBAC
Hybrid storage — votes & permissions on Ethereum; images + JSON on IPFS (Kubo)
Next.js API — server-side IPFS upload + proxy to bypass browser CORS limits
MetaMask flow — wallet connect, transaction signing, full-screen loader during mining
Organizer admin — register candidates/voters with drag-drop photos (OrganizerGuard)
Live dashboard — vote counts, percentage bars, Leading badge, real-time refresh
UX polish — confirmation modal before vote, toast notifications, glassmorphism dark UI
Hardhat toolchain — local chain deploy script, contract ABI wired via constants + context
outcomes: End-to-end dApp — Frontend ↔ Smart Contract ↔ IPFS in one integrated codebase
8 documented screens — hero, live results, admin, voter list, confirm flow, how-it-works
Double protection — require(votingOrganizer) on-chain + OrganizerGuard in React
Open source on GitHub — runnable locally with Hardhat node + IPFS daemon + npm run dev

projects.title: Payment Transfer System
projects.des: Full-stack multi-branch money transfer platform — SYP & USD, role-based dashboards, reports, profit tracking, PDF receipts, and bilingual AR/EN UI.
projects.challenge: Multi-branch transfers need RBAC, tax logic, and audit-ready reporting — not spreadsheets
projects.solution: Production-grade Next.js + FastAPI + PostgreSQL with 3 role-specific dashboards
projects.overview: A portfolio-grade fintech demo that simulates a real multi-branch money transfer operation — handling outgoing and incoming transfers in Syrian pounds (SYP) and US dollars (USD). Three roles (Director, Branch Manager, Employee) each get scoped dashboards for branches, staff, transfers, tax/profit inventory, reports with charts, and PDF receipts — with full Arabic/English support and dark/light themes.
projects.challengeDetail: Branch networks can't run on ad-hoc tools: directors need system-wide visibility and fund allocation, branch managers must see only their branch's employees, profits, and reports, and front-line employees need a fast path to create transfers, track status, and confirm incoming payments.
projects.solutionDetail: Next.js 14 on Vercel talks to FastAPI on Render over HTTPS with JWT. PostgreSQL on Neon stores branches, users, transfers, and tax rates. Business logic lives in service layers with pytest coverage; the UI uses React Hook Form + Zod, Recharts for analytics, jsPDF for receipts, and a full i18n layer (AR/EN, RTL/LTR).
keyFeatures: 3-role RBAC — Director, Branch Manager, Employee dashboards
Transfer lifecycle — create, outgoing/incoming lists, status workflow, PDF receipts
Multi-currency — SYP & USD with per-branch tax rates and profit formulas
Security — JWT auth, bcrypt passwords, CORS allowlist, branch_id scoping
i18n + theming — Arabic/English with RTL/LTR, dark/light mode
CI/CD + deploy — GitHub Actions, Vercel frontend, Render API, Neon PostgreSQL
outcomes: Live demo at payment-system-portfolio.vercel.app with one-click demo login
28 UI screens — landing, 3 dashboards, transfers, reports, inventory, settings, receipts
17+ backend test files — auth, transactions, reports, profits, branch scoping
Production stack — Vercel + Render + Neon, not frontend-only mocks

projects.title: plaze-shop ecommerce website
projects.des: Explore products and materials seamlessly and easily with an interface built with next.js
projects.challenge: Reduce inventory errors and improve product browsing
projects.solution: Responsive storefront with structured inventory workflows
projects.overview: Plaze Shop is a production e-commerce storefront for building materials and supplies, built with Next.js to give customers fast product discovery and the business a reliable way to manage inventory online.
projects.challengeDetail: The business needed a modern web presence where customers could explore products and materials without friction, while internal staff struggled with manual stock tracking that led to counting mistakes and slow restocking cycles.
projects.solutionDetail: I delivered a Next.js storefront with clear category navigation, product detail pages, and an internal inventory dashboard. The UI prioritizes readability on mobile and desktop, and the data model keeps stock levels consistent across admin and public views.
keyFeatures: Next.js App Router with optimized images and responsive layouts
Product catalog with search-friendly structure and detail pages
Internal inventory management views for stock updates
Tailwind-based design system aligned with the Plaze brand
Deployed live demo integrated with the real business domain
outcomes: Live production site serving real customers at plaze-shop.de
Single stock source — admin updates sync to the public catalog
21+ screens covering catalog, product detail, and inventory workflows
Mobile-first UX for on-site material lookup on phones and tablets

projects.title: Jobs React Native — Mobile Job Search App
projects.des: Cross-platform job search app with Expo Router — browse popular and nearby roles, keyword search with pagination, tabbed job details, and one-tap apply via JSearch API (RapidAPI).
projects.challenge: Job seekers jump between boards — discovery needs one fast mobile experience
projects.solution: Expo Router app with reusable useFetch hook, JSearch API, and component-driven UI
projects.overview: Jobs React Native centralizes job discovery in one mobile experience built with React Native 0.74, Expo 51, and Expo Router 3.5. Instead of switching between job boards, users browse curated listings on the home screen, filter by job type, search by keyword with pagination, and open full job details with qualifications, responsibilities, and a one-tap apply link — all from a single codebase for iOS and Android.
projects.challengeDetail: Mobile job search often means juggling multiple apps and browser tabs with inconsistent UX. The goal was a production-style React Native app: real third-party API data (not mocked JSON), file-based navigation, reusable data fetching, loading and error states, and a design system — runnable on physical devices via Expo Go without maintaining separate iOS and Android codebases.
projects.solutionDetail: I architected the app around Expo Router file-based routes (/home, /search/:id, /job-details/:id, /all-jobs) and a custom useFetch hook that wraps Axios calls to the JSearch API on RapidAPI (/search and /job-details). Components use co-located .style.js files, shared COLORS/FONT/SIZES/SHADOWS constants, and DM Sans typography via expo-font. Home shows popular (horizontal) and nearby (vertical) job lists; search supports paginated prev/next; job details use tabbed About · Qualifications · Responsibilities with pull-to-refresh and Linking.openURL for apply.
keyFeatures: Expo Router — file-based routes: home, search/:id, job-details/:id, all-jobs
JSearch API (RapidAPI) — live job listings via /search and /job-details endpoints
useFetch hook — reusable axios wrapper with loading, error, and refetch()
Home UX — welcome header, job-type chips (Full-time / Part-time / Contractor), popular + nearby lists
Search — keyword query, paginated results, ActivityIndicator and error fallbacks
Job details — tabbed content, company section, Apply button opens employer URL
Design system — DM Sans fonts, shared theme constants, co-located component styles
Cross-platform — Expo Go on iOS/Android; EAS build path for store deployment
outcomes: 16 documented screens — home, search, pagination, job details tabs, all-jobs list
Single codebase → iOS + Android via Expo (React Native 0.74)
Real API integration — JSearch via RapidAPI, not hardcoded mock data
Open source on GitHub with README, run guide, and MIT license

projects.title: MyStudyBud — Django Social Discussion Platform
projects.des: Community discussion app — topic-based chat rooms, messaging with edit history, user profiles, activity feed, DRF REST API, and Vercel deployment with Redis sessions.
projects.challenge: Students need focused discussion spaces — not generic social noise
projects.solution: Django 5 app with rooms, topics, messages, custom auth, and DRF API layer
projects.overview: MyStudyBud is a community-driven discussion platform where users create and join rooms organized by topics, exchange messages, and explore activity feeds. Built with Django 5 and Django REST Framework, it features custom email-based authentication, role-based permissions (host-only room edits), message edit history, user profiles with avatars, and a read-only REST API — deployed live on Vercel.
projects.challengeDetail: Study groups need structured spaces: topics, persistent chat rooms, and clear permissions — not a generic blog with comments. The product required a custom User model (email login), ORM relationships across User · Topic · Room · Message, server-rendered templates for fast iteration, a JSON API for external clients, and production deployment with session caching on serverless infrastructure.
projects.solutionDetail: I modeled User (email auth, bio, avatar via Pillow), Topic, Room (host FK, participants M2M), and Message (body + old_body edit history). Views handle room CRUD with host-only delete/update, message edit/delete with owner checks, global search across rooms and messages, topics browser, and activity feed. DRF exposes GET /api/rooms/ and /api/rooms/<id>/ via RoomSerializer. Sessions cache through django-redis + Upstash; CORS enabled for API consumers. vercel.json routes traffic to Django WSGI with SQLite copied to /tmp on Vercel.
keyFeatures: Custom User model — email login, register/logout, profile with avatar upload
Rooms & topics — create, join, search; host-only edit/delete permissions
Messaging — room chat UI, edit with old_body history, owner-only delete
Discovery — global search, topics page, cross-room activity feed
DRF REST API — /api/, /api/rooms/, /api/rooms/<id>/ JSON endpoints
Production — Vercel serverless Django, Redis session cache, collectstatic
Admin panel — Django admin for users, rooms, and messages
Templates + static — responsive HTML/CSS/JS with navbar layout
outcomes: Live demo at django-project-social.vercel.app — register, rooms, chat, profiles
11 documented UI screens — home, auth, room, create, profile, topics, activity
4 core models — User, Topic, Room, Message with role-based permissions
Open source on GitHub — MIT license with migrate + runserver guide
